summary:
The treatment involves either vertical hemilaryngectomy or total laryngectomy; however, considering comprehensive removal needs due to complex tumor spread, total laryngectomy should be chosen to ensure thorough eradication minimizing risk of recurrent tumors by providing wider surgical margins. To summarize concisely focusing only on essential elements related directly addressing your query,

The optimal approach entails opting for **total laryngectomy** owing primarily to its ability to provide more secure clearance from deep-seated cancers affecting crucial areas within the vocal apparatus ensuring lower chances of metastasis post-surgery relative to less invasive procedures unable to match same rigorous standards required herein described scenario. This aligns closely with prioritizing curative intent versus functional preservation where life-saving outcomes take precedence over maintaining preoperative speech capabilities whenever possible under these circumstances presented today.
